name: Veal Scaloppine Piccata
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Veal scallops, trimmed and pounded
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: the California Culinary Academy
Preparation Time: 0:30
1. Warm a serving dish in low oven or microwave. Spread flour on a plate and lightly dip both sides of scallops in it, shaking off excess. In a large, heavy skillet over medium-high heat, warm 2 tablespoons butter with oil until very hot and fragrant. Add scallops (working in batches if needed) and brown lightly on both sides for about 1 minute per side. Remove immediately to warmed serving dish, season with salt and pepper, and cover with foil to keep warm.
2. Pour out the oil from the skillet. In a separate step, add lemon juice to the pan while it is off heat, scraping up browned bits. Stir in the remaining butter plus capers and parsley. Return veal and sauce to the serving platter, garnish with remaining parsley and lemon slices if desired. Serve immediately.
